editor_in_loop_skipped: Drafted with rubric in mind; California sibling pattern proven through linter. Hawaii ABC test is the disjunctive form (HRS § 383-7) which differs from California's stricter Dynamex form; capsule + Prong B body prose reflect that softener. PHC overlay is the unique Hawaii layer and gets its own section.
high_concern_flags: verify_prompt_facts_applied: prompt asserted Hawaii uses ABC test under HRS § 383-7 for UI — verified against statute; ABC test wording is the older disjunctive form (off-premises OR outside usual course), not the strict Dynamex form. Body prose distinguishes this from California explicitly.

verify_prompt_facts_applied: prompt asserted PHC eligibility depends uniquely on employee status — confirmed: HRS ch. 393 trigger is hours per week + monthly earnings threshold tied to employee status; contractors are not eligible regardless of hours. Reclassification triggers back-premium order under § 393-33.

verify_prompt_facts_applied: cost figures cross-checked against hawaii.json compliance cache (UI wage base $64,500, TDI wage base $1,500.21 weekly, minimum wage $16.00 from 1 January 2026, PHC trigger $1,387 monthly).

spec_notes: Modelled on California sibling structure (master-0030) with Hawaii-specific data swaps.
ABC prong text quoted verbatim from HRS § 383-7 (the disjunctive form predating Dynamex).
PHC penalty bands and back-premium mechanics quoted from HRS § 393-33.
Three named scenarios (Kai, Leilani, Makoa) per editorial standard v2 requirement.
Stamp-block titled 'Three Tests, One Healthcare Bill' is the metaphor-light visual anchor; no body metaphor used elsewhere.
Disclaimer aside copy uses Hawaii-specific text per instruction.
No em-dashes in body prose. No forbidden words. Lead sentence ≤25 words.
Canonical CTAs use https://www.teamed.global/contact and https://www.teamed.global/tools/contractor-classification per global rule.
